AgentSentry
FeaturesHow It WorksROI CalculatorDemoDocsPricingGitHub

Documentation

Everything you need to get started with AgentSentry.

Quick Start

  1. 1

    Install

    npm install agent-sentry
  2. 2

    Initialize

    npx agent-sentry init --level 2
  3. 3

    Wire MCP

    {
  "mcpServers": {
    "agent-sentry": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["agent-sentry", "serve"]
    }
  }
}
  4. 4

    Verify

    npx agent-sentry health

Explore the Docs

MCP Integration

Connect AgentSentry to Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible client.

CLI Reference

11 commands for init, health, metrics, search, streaming, and more.

Configuration

Customize enablement levels, storage providers, embedding backends, and more.

Security

Secret detection patterns, rules validation, and risk scoring details.

Storage Providers

SQLite (default), Supabase (beta), and custom provider interface.

Plugin System

Build custom plugins with manifest-based discovery and hook lifecycle.

CLI Reference

AgentSentry ships with 11 CLI commands covering initialization, observability, memory, plugins, and maintenance.

CommandDescription
initInitialize AgentSentry in a project with interactive or flag-based setup.
healthRun a health check on the AgentSentry installation and dependencies.
metricsDisplay aggregated metrics for events, sessions, and risk scores.
memory searchSemantic search across the hash-chained memory store.
memory listList recent memory entries with optional filters.
streamStream real-time events from the AgentSentry event bus.
plugin listList installed plugins and their activation status.
config showDisplay the current AgentSentry configuration.
dashboardLaunch the local observability dashboard in your browser.
enableInteractive wizard to change the progressive enablement level.
pruneRemove stale sessions, expired events, and orphaned memory entries.
AgentSentry

Memory-aware agent safety. Your AI agents never forget — and never go rogue.
